behavior_summary: Provides the object's response when the player inspects or 'looks at' the cauldron: it displays a single short text/bark 'cauldron' and then issues a suspend.
There is no branching, menu, or external call behavior; the handler is a minimal U8P_CAULDRON::look/inspection routine.
The routine is intended as an object label/short description rather than a dialogue interaction.
notable_control_flow: U8P_CAULDRON::look executes, emits one Bark node with text 'cauldron', then issues a suspend to end or pause the U8P_CAULDRON::look process.
No conditional logic or menu/choice presentation is present; control flow is linear and immediate.
open_questions: Is U8P_CAULDRON::look invoked by the explicit player Look/Examine action, by entering a fast-area trigger, or both? (metadata suggests either; exact trigger mapping is not present).
What exact engine behavior does the suspend perform here (terminate process, yield to engine, or wait for player dismiss) in this project context?
The raw data contained Unknown nodes around the bark — do those represent bookkeeping or additional descriptive text that was elided?

main_function.name: U8P_CAULDRON::look
main_function.purpose: Serve as the U8P_CAULDRON::look/inspection handler for the cauldron object: present the object's short description label to the player and then suspend the process.
entry_conditions: Inferred: called when the player uses the Look/Examine action on the cauldron.
Inferred: may also be invoked when an actor enters the object's U8P_CAULDRON::look-type fast-area trigger (processType 0x0208 indicates a U8P_CAULDRON::look/inspection process).

function_map.name: U8P_CAULDRON::look
function_map.role: U8P_CAULDRON::look/inspection handler that emits a short object label and suspends

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the object's response when the player inspects or 'looks at' the cauldron: it displays a single short text/bark 'cauldron' and then issues a suspend.
